title: Boat Rental in Mombasa: A Budget Guide for 2026
topic: a budget guide to renting a boat in Mombasa
html:
Mombasa offers affordable boat rentals starting from around EUR 100 for a half-day trip, with departures from Nyali Beach and the Likoni Ferry channel. Compare verified options on BoatHire24 to find a vessel that suits your budget and itinerary.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Mombasa?

Prices range from EUR 30 per person for shared tours to EUR 800 for a full-day crewed yacht. Shared dhow trips to Wasini Island cost about EUR 40 per person, while private half-day dinghy hire from Nyali Beach starts at EUR 100. A full-day catamaran charter with skipper and lunch is typically EUR 400-600, and luxury motor yachts such as the Prestige 500 command EUR 700-800 for a day. Always confirm fuel and crew gratuity in advance.

Where to set off in Mombasa

Most boat hire departs from Mombasa Marina, a protected basin near the city centre. Nyali Beach is a popular launch point for smaller skiffs and watersports. English Point, at the entrance to Mombasa Harbour, provides easy access to the offshore reefs. The Likoni Channel, connecting Mombasa Island to the south mainland, is the main thoroughfare for charters heading to Diani Beach. For longer trips, Kilifi Creek, about 50 km north, offers a sheltered anchorage and a scenic mangrove cruise. Wasini Island, south of Mombasa, is a common destination for snorkelling and dolphin spotting, approximately 10 nautical miles from Shimoni. Tudor Creek, on the north side of the island, is ideal for quiet kayaking and birdwatching.

Which boat to rent

Consider these options when browsing skippered charters in Mombasa.

  1. Traditional dhow, best for cultural groups of up to 12 guests, from EUR 40 per person for a half-day cruise.
  2. Small motorboat, best for private fishing or island hopping with 4-6 guests, from EUR 100 for a half-day.
  3. Catamaran, best for family days with 10-15 guests, from EUR 450 for a full-day charter with picnic.
  4. Luxury motor yacht, best for corporate or special events with 8-12 guests, from EUR 700 for a full-day, including crew and nibbles. For details, see the Prestige 500 charter page.

Cost breakdown for boat hire in Mombasa

  • Shared dhow tour (half-day, per person): EUR 30-50
  • Private small motorboat (half-day, 4 people): EUR 100-150
  • Catamaran (full-day, up to 10 guests): EUR 400-600
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full-day, up to 8 guests): EUR 700-800

All prices in EUR as of 2026. Fuel, skipper, and lunch are typically included for crewed charters but verify before booking.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Mombasa?

No licence is required for skippered rentals; only a valid ID is needed. For bareboat hire, a recognised powerboat qualification (such as the RYA Powerboat Level 2) is usually mandatory. The operator will confirm exact requirements at the time of booking. The best season is from December to March, when the northeast monsoon (Kaskazi) brings calm seas and sunny weather. The long rains in April-May often disrupt trips, while the southeast monsoon (Kusi) from June to October can produce stronger winds.

An early-morning departure from Mombasa Marina gives you a better chance of calm water and dolphin sightings near the channel entrance before the sea breeze picks up around midday.

At a glance

  • Price range: EUR 30-800 per trip
  • Peak months: December to March
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Maximum capacity: up to 15 guests on larger vessels
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Mombasa

Can I rent a boat for just a few hours in Mombasa?

Yes, many operators offer two-hour sunset cruises from Nyali Beach or Mombasa Marina for around EUR 60-80 per group.

Is it safe to rent a boat independently in Mombasa?

Stick to reputable platforms such as BoatHire24, which verifies listed vessels and operators, and always check the safety equipment onboard.

What marine life can I expect to see near Mombasa?

Common sightings include dolphins, sea turtles, and humpback whales during the August-September migration.

Are there overnight boat charter options in Mombasa?

Some catamaran and yacht charters offer overnight trips to Wasini Island or Lamu, with sleeping cabins for up to six guests.

To plan your budget-friendly trip, explore options on the BoatHire24 marketplace. If you own a boat in Mombasa, list it to reach thousands of travellers planning their 2026 holiday.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Mombasa and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
